_label_note: US Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered role/legal mailbox bait, public privacy/ethics helplines, generic training-list wording, negated material-adverse-effect wording, Reg FD/10b/Phase shorthand amount bait, placeholder account IDs, entity registry numbers, and public/generic material-event guidance.
_label_tier_note: US Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

birthday 03/15/1987, internal extension 4427, Denvale badge ID D-CA-0113, and prior device sync at 21:02

must_not_detect.reason: Aggregate demographic statistic not identifying a person
Truncated aggregate ZIP code, not linkable to an individual
Partial birth date without linkage; weak identifier not PII alone
Partial account info without linkage; weak identifier not PII alone
Generic device model; weak identifier not PII alone
City/state alone; weak identifier not PII alone
Device serial without linkage to a person

uncertain.reason: Internal phone extension can be linkable but is not covered by enumerated PII rules
Work location can contribute to identifiability when combined but not explicitly covered alone
Granular timestamp can aid linkage but not directly covered by allowed PII rules
Login/sync time can aid linkage but lacks a specific rule
City/state for a household is quasi-identifying but not covered as a standalone rule
Identifies a sell-side analyst firm relevant to Reg FD context but not mapped to a current MNPI rule
